dealSummary: San Francisco-based marketing analytics platform developer AppsFlyer secured a $1 billion Series E from investors including Moloco, Google and Meta at a $2.7 billion valuation.
Berlin-based drone startup Stark received a €500 million round from investors including Founders Fund and Sequoia at a €3.2 billion valuation.
Osanni Bio, a drug discovery biotech, secured a $190 million Series B led by Patient Square Capital.
Clinical AI startup Cadence, which specializes in chronic disease care, raised a $100 million Series C led by Spark Capital.
Blockchain data platform developer Allium secured a $40 million Series B led by Amplify Partners.
Tsuga, a Paris-based enterprise observability startup, raised a $35 million Series A led by Singular.
Attention, the developer of AI sales and revenue tools, received a $30 million Series B led by RTP Global.
Payroll and benefits platform developer Niural raised $21 million from investors including FOG Ventures and NewView Capital to add to its Series A.
leadInvestors: FOG Ventures, NewView Capital to add to its Series A

dealSummary: Biotech startup Rapalogix Health, which specializes in cellular longevity in dermatology, secured a $20 million Series A led by GordonMD Global Investments and Woodline Partners.
Berlin-based insulation startup Varm raised a €17.5 million Series A led by ABN AMRO Sustainable Impact Fund.
French semiconductor specialist AlpSemi received €17 million in a round led by Yotta Capital Partners.
Tetrix, which develops an investment platform for limited partners, raised a $15 million Series A led by White Star Capital and Innovation Endeavors.
UK-based biotech group Trimtech Therapeutics raised $14 million in a round led by JJDC and BGF, bringing its seed round to $47 million.
French fleet leasing startup Flease secured €13 million in a round led by Partech.
Timefold, the Belgian maker of a developer platform for vehicle routing and shift scheduling APIs, raised a $13 million Series A led by Alstin Capital.
